top_review:
I visited Saung Suluh in Baturaden and had a really pleasant time. I ordered the nasi goreng and liked it very much, especially because it has a nice smoky flavor, which suits my taste. The food overall is satisfying and enjoyable.
While waiting for the food, there are many activities you can do, such as playing football, feeding the fish, or even enjoying fish therapy, so you won’t get bored. They also offer homestay accommodation, which is a nice option if you want to stay longer and enjoy the area.
The atmosphere is calm and surrounded by nature, making the whole experience relaxing. Overall, it’s a good place with good food, lots of activities, and a pleasant environment.

top_review:
Well decorated cafe. Lots of menu variant. And surprisingly, all of them was great. This cafe also has indoor smoking area, which quite unusual but become its plus point.
I ordered:
1x Dimsum (Siomay): Very soft and delicious!
1x Salted Egg Tofu: Surprisingly delicious!
1x Pan Seared Salmon: Big cut of salmon meat & delicious cream sauce. If only the mashed potatoes a bit softer, it would be perfect!
1x Premium Tea (Camomile): Dilmah tea, love it
1x Quattro’s Bowl: A bowl of mixed fruit topped with matcha ice cream & almond. The soup itself was too plain, I thought it would be pure creamy yoghurt/pure plain milk, but it’s not.

Sunday: 10:00 AM – 11:00 PM
top_review:
This restaurant is considered upscale by locals and is a popular restaurant for special occasions due to its location. The restaurant is quite large with many seats inside and outside. It has a special platform with a glass floor which is popular for taking photos with a view.
The staff is very friendly and accommodating but also made quite a few mistakes with our order. The check was also incorrect and one dish too many added.
The menu is quite big, but we didn’t find the quality of the food to match the prices.
The wagyu steak was not very tender and the flavor was odd. It is one of the most expensive dishes on the menu, but we would not order it again. In fact, the much cheaper beef satay was far better.
We were a bigger group and tried many dishes, but nobody felt that that dishes were of very good quality.
The restrooms are not very clean.
All in all, this is an okay restaurant which lives off of its location and the view, and not because of the dining experience.

top_review:
I recently visited AT:NINE, hoping for a pleasant dining experience, but unfortunately, it left much to be desired.

From the start, the service was disappointing. The order taker seemed rude and impatient, constantly asking, "Anything else?" in a rushed tone. It felt like they wanted us to place our order as quickly as possible, leaving no room for us to make decisions at a comfortable pace. To make things worse, there were no waiters to deliver the drinks to the table, which felt like a letdown for the price we were paying.

Given the service and overall experience, the food did not feel worth the price. While the menu might look appealing, the actual value of the meal fell short of expectations.

The place itself is nice, with modern décor and a good layout. However, the air conditioning was poorly managed. Even though it was supposedly set to 16°C, it felt closer to 30°C inside, making the environment uncomfortable.

Glad I did, coz the cafe's ambience is so cozy.
Could imagine just chillin here and doing some work😄

Staffs were friendly and the menu were all interesting and mouth watering.
Nasi Kuring was the main highlight fer sure, rice that is fragrant, fried chicken cooked perfectly,and the chilli...mmmm mmmmmmm good‼️

That and the Capucinno were both perfect companions to the day.

Would definitely return whenever I visit Purwokerto again👌🏻

top_review:
Hi, I am Zico.
I am going to review a café namely My Story Resto.

My Story Resto is located in Purwokerto.

It’s a modern and cozy café that serves various types of food and beverages.

This café is well known for its tasty food, friendly service, and affordable price.

The interior wall of this café is well and beautifully decorated with plants, warm lights, and wooden furniture.

My favorite spot is the corner seat near the window.

I recently dined there as my friend advised me to try this place.

For the appetizer, I ordered French fries.
It’s crispy and salty in a good way. It was delicious!

For the main course, I ordered grilled chicken with rice.
It’s juicy and flavorful. It was so good!

The ambience is truly relaxing and perfect for hanging out with friends.

Overall, I would really recommend My Story resto to anyone

Overall, i dont think i would recomend this place to anyone

name: Bydans Coffee Bar
address: Gg. Masjid, Mangunjaya, Purwokerto, Kec. Purwokerto Tim., Kabupaten Banyumas, Jawa Tengah 53114, Indonesia
top_review: Tried one of the vintage coffee shops in Purwokerto. I love the vibes, and there are two cats named Giri and Nunung. The open space isn’t hot and feels refreshing. There’s also an AC room, but I could smell the cat litter, so maybe that can be improved. I ordered a dirty latte with an extra shot of espresso. Even though they use a Flair, the espresso tastes just as good as one made with an espresso machine. The dirty latte is good too—the milk becomes really sweet, though for my taste it’s a bit too sweet. The barista is very kind and humble. I’ll definitely come back when I visit Purwokerto again.

top_review:
WARMINDO BURJO ASEP
A comfortable place for college students on the first or last day of the month without emptying their wallets. This is my go-to place on the last day of the month because eating rice and eggs here is only 7,000 rupiah, a cheap and cheerful choice to overcome financial problems during the lean season. They sell a variety of dishes, from Nastel Orak Arik Magelangan Mirebus Midokdok and others, all at pocket-friendly prices for students (a.k.a. mwurahhh). The place is quite spacious, not only with tables in the front, but also with a seating area next to it, so you can chat with friends more freely. This place has been open since around 2010, if I'm not mistaken, and it's a place that has stood the test of time. The food is delicious, you can request anything you like. There's a TV here that the owner often uses to play PS2, but since he got bored, he no longer uses it for PS2.

top_review:
MILLENNIAL DURIAN SHAKE

Located in front of the Faculty of Social and Political Sciences (Fisip) of Unsoed, the place is quite nice and modern, with its distinctive yellow color, the durian flavor, and ample parking. They offer various varieties of durian shakes, as well as durian pancakes.

I initially bought this place after wandering around the Unsoed area, unsure of what to eat, and decided to buy the Millenial Durian Shake. The price is quite affordable, the cup is large, and the filling is quite generous, especially the ice cubes. After trying it, it was indeed delicious; the durian ice tasted like ice, although I tasted more ice cubes. However, I was a bit disappointed. After a while, I struggled to drink it, but I realized that the straw I used was a regular drinking straw, not a large one, so it took a lot of effort to suck the durian in.
